codeReview.summary: The PUT /api/interviews/:id implementation follows the intended auth, ownership, ID parsing, and field-whitelist flow for normal object payloads, but its request-body validation is incomplete. The handler assumes c.req.json() returns an object, so a non-object JSON payload can bypass the intended 400 validation path and crash the route instead.

issues.description: The handler reads `body.title` and `body.transcript` immediately after `await c.req.json()`. If the client sends valid JSON `null`, `body` is null and those property reads throw before the 400 validation runs, producing a 500 instead of the required validation error for missing fields. Add a body-shape guard (and ideally safe JSON parse handling) before accessing properties.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit cef734b diff, and current API route/test files. Result: fail, because the new PUT handler can crash on a non-object JSON payload instead of returning the required 400 validation response; no shared-state documentation gaps stood out during this review.
